question_title: How many EU member states will the AI Act National Implementation tracker classify as having clear market surveillance and notifying authorities on August 31, 2026?
summary: The FLI AI Act National Implementation tracker is an editorially-maintained step function, not a real-time metric. Wayback Machine data shows it held at 3 Clear for ~13 months (May 2025 → June 16, 2026), even through the Aug 2, 2025 legal deadline, then jumped to 9 Clear in a June 17, 2026 overhaul, still 9 on July 3, 2026. The question resolves on the count when Metaculus checks on/after Aug 31, 2026. The dominant scenario is no further refresh (status quo 9 → ≤9), given the team just overhauled the page ~3 weeks ago and historically ignored deadlines. The counter-scenario is a refresh around the Aug 2, 2026 applicability milestone (a bigger news hook than Aug 2, 2025), flipping some of the 12 Partial states to Clear and landing in 10-13. I estimate ~40% chance of a count-changing refresh; conditional on it, the count likely lands 10-13 with a thin tail to 14-15. Reaching >15 (+7 in one refresh) is implausible.

factors.description: Current tracker value is 9 Clear; status quo resolves ≤9. Tracker is a sticky step-function that sat unchanged at 3 for 13 months.
Team just did a full overhaul June 17 2026 (3→9); post-overhaul pages tend to stay stable for months, reducing chance of another refresh before the Aug 31 check.
Team did NOT refresh around the prior Aug 2 2025 deadline despite it passing — deadlines historically do not reliably trigger tracker updates.
12 states currently 'Partial' — if a refresh occurs, several could flip to Clear, favoring the 10-13 range.
Aug 2 2026 is the major AI Act high-risk/governance applicability milestone (bigger news hook than 2025) that could trigger a count-raising refresh in the Aug 2-31 window.

reasoning:
Forecast submitted.

**How many EU member states will the tracker classify as "Clear" on Aug 31, 2026?**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| **≤9** | **63%** |
| 10 or 11 | 15% |
| 12 or 13 | 12% |
| 14 or 15 | 7% |
| >15 | 3% |

**Reasoning:** The tracker is an editorially-maintained step function that held at 3 Clear for 13 months before jumping to 9 in a June 17, 2026 overhaul (still 9 as of July 3). The dominant scenario is no further refresh before Metaculus checks (→ ≤9). The main upside risk is a refresh timed to the Aug 2, 2026 AI Act applicability milestone flipping some of the 12 "Partial" states to Clear, which would land in the 10-13 range. The premortem reviewer approved the forecast with no forecast-affecting errors.
sources_consulted: [Overview of all AI Act National Implementation Plans](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/national-implementation-plans/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the number of National Competent Authorities classified as "Clear" at the EU's  [Overview of all AI Act National Implementation Plans](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/national-implementation-plans/) tracker, when checked by Metaculus on or after August 31, 2026.

input.anchor: Status quo = current tracker value of 9 Clear (source: artificialintelligenceact.eu tracker, verified July 3 2026 via Wayback). Base: ≤9 favored by status-quo persistence.
input.assessment: The tracker is an editorially-maintained step function, not a real-time metric. It held at 3 Clear for 13 months, then jumped to 9 on June 17 2026 and remains 9. The question resolves on the count when Metaculus checks on/after Aug 31. The dominant scenario is no further refresh (status quo 9 → ≤9), given the team just overhauled the page ~3 weeks ago and historically ignored the Aug 2 2025 deadline. Counter-scenario: a refresh around the Aug 2 2026 applicability milestone bumps some of the 12 Partial states to Clear, landing in 10-13. I estimate ~40% chance of a count-changing refresh; conditional on a refresh, the count likely lands 10-13 with a tail to 14-15.
input.tool_audit: fetch_url gave current tracker state (9 Clear). research() sub-agent provided excellent Wayback Machine trajectory confirming the step-function behavior and exact historical counts. Both highly useful, no failures.
input.process_reflection: The research sub-agent's Wayback Machine trajectory reconstruction was exactly what this question needed — it turned a naive 'project the trend' problem into a well-understood editorial-update model. Scaffolding worked well.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
The forecast is well-grounded and I found no forecast-affecting errors. Detailed verification:

FACTORS — all supported by the trace:
- F1 (≤9, status quo 9, sat at 3 for 13 months): Confirmed. Wayback snapshots show 3 Clear from the 2 Aug 2025 capture through 16 Jun 2026 (~13 months), then 9. Logit correctly positive toward ≤9.
- F2 (June 17 2026 overhaul, 3→9): Confirmed — transition bounded precisely between 16 Jun (still 3) and 17 Jun (9) snapshots.
- F3 (Aug 2 2026 milestone could trigger refresh): Grounded; Aug 2 2026 is confirmed as the major high-risk applicability/penalties deadline. Logit correctly signed toward higher buckets.
- F4 (12 Partial states as pipeline): Confirmed (9/12/6 split verified across multiple fetches).
- F5 (team did NOT refresh around Aug 2 2025 deadline): Confirmed — the 2 Aug 2025 snapshot still showed "19 May 2025 / 3 Clear." Correctly signed toward ≤9.

No hallucinated evidence, no double-counting (each factor draws on a distinct aspect of the trajectory), no wrong-direction factors, no contradiction between assessment and factors.

RESOLUTION CRITERIA: Provided and correctly engaged — the factors and assessment target the exact FLI tracker "Clear" count on/after Aug 31, using the authoritative source. No misalignment.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: The June 17 2026 overhaul (pre-publication) is correctly used as the current status-quo anchor (9), not as already resolving a forward-looking count. The question asks for the Aug 31 value; the agent builds forward-looking refresh probability. Not a Case-2 violation.

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate aligns with the agent's. Base rate of tracker refreshes is very low (~2 major revisions in ~20 months), strongly favoring status-quo ≤9, but the Aug 2 2026 deadline is a genuinely larger news hook than Aug 2 2025 and falls in the Aug 2–31 window just before the Metaculus check, justifying a meaningful refresh probability. The agent's ~40% count-changing-refresh estimate and distribution (≤9: 63%, 10-11: 15%, 12-13: 12%, 14-15: 7%, >15: 3%) is well-calibrated; the >15 tail is appropriately thin (would require +7 in one refresh). I would not move this materially.

SELF-EXAMINATION: The counterargument is strong — it constructs a coherent alternative (dormancy reflects an abandoned methodology, not current cadence; Aug 2 2026 creates strong refresh incentive; 12 Partial states could push modal outcome to 12-13). Confidence 0.60 appropriately reflects genuine uncertainty about editorial cadence.

MINOR (non-blocking) ISSUE: The tool-audit narrative says research was used with "no failures," but ground-truth metrics show the research tool had 1 error (a schema-validation failure on the first call, immediately retried successfully). This is a minor overstatement of tool reliability; it does not affect the forecast since the recovery produced complete data. Worth noting but not warn-worthy given the immediate successful recovery and otherwise excellent research quality.

Worldview check: the matching research entry (history-of-the-artificialintelligenceact-eu-nation-7187597f) is this session's own research, fresh, and fully consistent with the factors. No numeric sub-forecast on the same quantity and no contradictory entries exist.
